questions: Should clicking a shot group label select all its clips (replacing current selection), or should it toggle/add to existing selection (shift-click behavior)?
Should the agent system prompt explicitly instruct the LLM to prefer same-shot operations when all selected clips share a shot, or is the structured context sufficient for the LLM to infer this?

For the 'place-after-selected' part of the duplicate workflow — should the agent automatically place the duplicated clip after the last clip in the same shot group, or after the specific selected clip? (These differ when a shot has multiple clips and only one is selected.)

success_criteria.criterion: describeTimeline() output includes a 'Shot groups:' section listing each pinned shot group with shotId, shotName, trackId, clipIds, and mode when pinnedShotGroups exist in config
Each clip line in describeTimeline() includes shot=<shotName> when the clip belongs to a pinned shot group
SelectedClipPayload accepts optional shot_id and shot_name fields without breaking existing consumers
buildSelectedClipsPrompt() includes shot info in selected clip lines when shot_id/shot_name are present
Clicking a shot group label in the timeline selects all clips in that group
Frontend agent invocation payload includes shot_id and shot_name for clips that belong to pinned shot groups
executeDuplicateGeneration() uses shot_id from selectedClips when available, skipping the findShotForGenerations DB lookup
All existing tests in supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/ continue to pass
New unit tests cover: describeTimeline with/without shot groups, buildSelectedClipsPrompt with shot context, duplicate-generation with shot_id from payload
New frontend test covers shot group click-to-select in TimelineCanvas
System prompt mentions shot context guidance for the LLM
Manual smoke test: select shot group → send agent message → verify agent response references the shot context

assumptions: Single-click on a shot group label selects all clips in that group, replacing the current selection. Shift-click would add to selection.
Shot names are available from the pinnedShotGroups config + a shots lookup (already done in useShotGroups). The agent-side can resolve names from the DB if not passed from the frontend.
The existing SelectedClipPayload type is shared between frontend and edge function — adding optional fields is backwards-compatible.
The describeTimeline() function currently receives only config and registry. Adding pinnedShotGroups info requires no signature change since pinnedShotGroups is already part of TimelineConfig.
The duplicate/edit/place workflow described in the system prompt (prompts.ts:154-158) does not need new tools — it needs better context so the existing tools work reliably with shot semantics.
Tests will use Vitest (already configured in the project) and follow existing test patterns in loop.test.ts and tool-calls.test.ts.
